name: Claude Runtime Anti-Drift Policy
scope: All live Claude runtime, verification, generated-truth, and operator-facing control-plane surfaces under the Claude home.
principles: Executable contracts are stronger than prose-only guidance.
Generated truth is authoritative for runtime existence questions.
One canonical path layer must anchor all Claude-home path construction.
Operator diagnostics must report effective runtime state, not misleading raw counts.
Cross-runtime references are architectural failures unless explicitly allowlisted as legacy bridges.

rules.statement: No live Claude runtime module may hardcode Claude-home or sibling-runtime absolute paths outside the canonical path authority.
settings.json must be rendered from the canonical hook registry rather than edited as handwritten runtime truth.
mcp.json must be rendered from the canonical launcher-backed MCP registry and optional capabilities must declare activation env.
Runtime inventory and summary artifacts must be generated and kept fresh after any live runtime change.
Repository health must aggregate through scripts/doctor.py, with any legacy execution entrypoint kept as an explicit compatibility shim only.
Live Claude runtime files must not reference Antigravity-owned paths or historical archive roots except through declared bridge surfaces.
Doctor must summarize effective gateway and MCP activation state rather than warning forever on capabilities that policy already disables.
No Claude architecture score claim may be accepted until targeted tests, generated-truth checks, invariants, and doctor have passed.

mergeGates: generated settings and mcp config are fresh
releaseGates: doctor passes with zero blocking failures
